claims.text: The Adam paper (Kingma & Ba, 2014) specifies good default settings for the tested machine learning problems as β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.999 and ε = 10^(−8), presented as paper-wide recommended defaults accompanying Algorithm 1.
claims.verification_reason: The passage directly states "Good default settings for the tested machine learning problems are α = 0.001, β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.999 and ε = 10^(−8)" as part of Algorithm 1, supporting every qualifier.

claims.text: The Transformer paper (Vaswani et al., 2017) reports using the Adam optimizer with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98 and ε = 10^(−9) in its optimizer-configuration description.
claims.verification_reason: The passage directly states "We used the Adam optimizer [20] with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98 and ε = 10^(−9)" in section 5.3 Optimizer, supporting the exact wording.

claims.text: The Adam paper's β2 = 0.999 is described as a paper-wide recommended default for the tested machine learning problems, whereas the Transformer paper reports β2 = 0.98 as the Adam setting it used in its experiments. Since one value is a general suggested default and the other is an experimental setting, they are not in direct conflict.
claims.verification_reason: The Adam passage directly supports "paper-wide recommended default for the tested machine learning problems." The Transformer passage supports that β2=0.98 is what "We used," indicating an experimental setting.
